channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.description: Building an AI agent sounds complicated. It's not. By the end of this guide, you'll have a working agent that can search the web, remember conversations, and handle multi-step tasks. No...

item.title: AI Test Automation Is Useful, but It Is Not Magic
item.description: AI has made test automation more exciting. It has also made it easier to fool ourselves. A generated test can look impressive in a demo. A self-healing locator can make a failure disappear. An AI...

item.title: I rebuilt a Texas city's open data portal in 30 minutes with Claude Code
item.description: The City of Kyle, TX has an open data portal. That already puts it ahead of most cities its size: budgets, investment reports, police data, water utilities, all published. But like a lot of...

item.title: AI 自进化与"发现模型"：RSI、验证与科学品味
item.description: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VGIbhIW5ljk 整文标题：《AI 自进化与"发现模型"：Anthropic 八成代码自写背后，Apodex 两位一线研究员谈 RSI、验证与科学品味》 第（一）部分｜开场引入：Anthropic 承认 80% 代码自写，RSI 成为硅谷热词（约 0% – 6%） 1 本期话题缘起：Anthropic 承认目前 80%...

item.description: When teams start with test automation, they usually ask the tool question too early. Should we use Playwright? Selenium? Cypress? A no-code platform? Should QA own it? Should developers write...

item.title: Microsoft lays off nearly 5,000 employees across Xbox, commercial sales
item.description: Microsoft cut around 4,800 roles, or 2.1% of its global workforce, on Monday — the latest in a series of layoffs that’s stoking fears of AI replacing jobs. The layoffs will hit Xbox and commercial...

item.title: I Run 1,000 Agents in Production. Here's What I Learned About Reliability
item.description: The 3 AM Wake-Up Call Last month, my phone buzzed at 3:17 AM. A Slack alert informed me that my OpenAI bill had jumped from $47 to $5,847 in 58 minutes. A while True loop without a termination...

item.title: Streaming Responses in Next.js App Router — Server-Sent Events and ReadableStream
item.description: Your API Isn't Slow. Your Response Strategy Is. There's a class of bug that never shows up in your performance benchmarks but destroys user trust every single time: the frozen UI on a long-running...

item.title: Nintendo will stop selling the original Switch in Europe next year
item.description: Nintendo is making a new version of the Switch 2 with a replaceable battery in Europe - but its predecessor has a very different future. As part of an updated FAQ about revisions to Nintendo...

item.title: Microsoft is selling off four Xbox studios as part of significant gaming cuts
item.description: Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees today, and more than 30 percent of the job losses are in the company's Xbox division. The significant gaming cuts will affect nearly every part of Xbox and...

item.title: Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees
item.description: A year after cutting around 9,100 employees, Microsoft is making further layoffs today as it begins its new financial year. The software maker is laying off around 4,800 employees today,...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Proxy Botnets, Browser Ransomware, AI Agent Tricks, Fake PoC Malware and More
item.description: A streaming box should not need a threat model. Neither should a username field, a demo repo, a reset flow, or a browser permission prompt. That is the irritating part this week: the risky pieces...

item.title: The Download: South Korea’s hottest bachelors, and advancing eye transplants
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. South Korea’s hottest new bachelors are chip workers Baek,...

item.description: A suspected China-nexus threat activity cluster has been observed targeting Indian taxpayers, tax professionals, and corporate finance teams to deliver a remote access trojan designed to steal...

item.title: France to Stop Certifying Non-Quantum-Safe Encryption
item.description: France is accelerating its transition to post-quantum encryption: France’s cybersecurity agency ANSSI said on Tuesday it would stop certifying security products that lack quantum-resistant...

item.title: South Korea’s hottest new bachelors are chip workers

item.title: New TrojPix Attack Leaks Data From Air-Gapped Systems via Video Cable Emissions
item.description: Researchers at Shandong University have shown a fast new way to pull data off computers that are cut off from every network. The technique, called TrojPix, tweaks on-screen pixels in ways the eye...

item.title: New Java-Based QuimaRAT MaaS Built to Run on Windows, Linux, and macOS
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a novel Java-based remote access trojan (RAT) called QuimaRAT that's capable of targeting Windows, Linux, and macOS environments. According to LevelBlue, the...

item.title: Opera GX Flaw Let Malicious Sites Auto-Install Mods to Steal Data From Visited Pages
item.description: Researchers found a flaw in Opera GX, the gaming-focused version of the Opera browser, that let a malicious website silently install a browser add-on and use it to lift specific data from the...

item.title: SkillCloak Lets Malicious AI Agent Skills Evade Static Scanners with Self-Extracting Packing
item.description: Scanners meant to catch malicious add-on "skills" for AI coding agents can be fooled by a few simple changes that leave the malware working, according to a new study from researchers at the Hong...
